title: D1-004d1 admission ledger core
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d1 admission ledger core: add the durable Postgres admission ledger foundation — Drizzle migration/schema, admissionLedger.ts, session advisory fence, and CLI destructive-diff read — keyed by host, binding, and execution identity. Commit before effects, use advisory fencing, and fail closed on mismatch or commit failure. This is the reusable admission ledger core; it does not own consumer wiring or rollback journaling.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. ≤400 net lines. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. Scope is narrowed to the ledger core only: Drizzle migration/schema + admissionLedger.ts + session advisory fence + CLI destructive-diff read. D1-004d2 owns Pi first-effect admission consumers; D1-004d3 owns the WorkspaceBridge operation admission seam. D1-004e owns append-only rollback-journal creation, prepared-state reads, and recovery fencing. D1-005c is the first concrete stable-core consumer of the admission hook.

## Explicit exclusions
No rollback-journal reads or writes, publication pointer, served acknowledgement, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. No Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, slash/reload hook wiring, or WorkspaceBridge operation wiring (those are D1-004d2/d3).

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit tests prove admission rows and advisory fencing commit before the first effect and reject a mismatch/commit failure without effects. Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
Focused proof verifies: admitted binding remains non-removable after process restart and revision-directory cleanup; landing-only revisions preserve execution identity; binding-id reuse with different execution facts fails closed; concurrent admission is idempotent; kill-after-commit-before-effect leaves a deliberate permanent fence; and pre-commit/lock failure produces no effect. No rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

Owned stable codes: D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

Split per sizing audit 2026-07-13; siblings D1-004d2/d3 carry the consumer seams (pre-declared in PR-PLAN).

description:

## Scope

SIZE FLAG (audit 2026-07-13): at the 400-line ceiling — if implementation overflows, STOP and pre-declare a 2-child stacked split before pushing.

Proof verifies a stopped one-shot migration container has DB-only access, non-root UID, read-only root, no state mounts, no privilege/capabilities, exact approved command/env, zero exit, and no secret canary in Docker inspect/config. An isolated-copy rehearsal proves both the new and immediately prior approved cores can boot and run read/write smoke after an expand-only migration; incompatible/contracting migrations reject before live DB change; observed drift quarantines the core with ingress stopped.

Owned stable codes: D1_ACTIVE_BINDING_RESTART_REQUIRED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).
notes: 2026-07-13: dispatched after oi8/#744 merge. Hard 400-net ceiling: audit current main and implementation plan before edits; if full acceptance cannot fit, stop before push and pre-declare exactly 2 stacked child beads. title: D1-005b1 approved migration compatibility evidence
description:
Goal: produce authenticated, reusable migration compatibility evidence before any live database or host mutation.

Scope: extend the root-owned ApprovedD1HostRelease authority with a bounded immediately-previous core identity (exact canonical digest-pinned ref; nullable only for first boot), never caller-owned. Consume the authenticated release and inspect approved current/previous images through the Docker daemon with registered runsc/systrap. Build a deterministic stopped one-shot migration specification with exact approved command/user/env and hardening. On an isolated database copy, run the expand-only migration rehearsal, boot both immediately previous and new approved cores, and prove read/write smoke. Mint non-forgeable compatibility evidence binding release, prior/current image IDs, migration digest/epoch, and rehearsal result.

Exclusions: no live database mutation; no live migration start; no core/ingress host creation/start; no publication/pointer/admission/P6-R; no custom launcher.

Acceptance/proof: focused codec/authority/parser/argv/redaction tests plus Docker-host isolated-copy proof. Previous image is authenticated by root authority; first-boot null is explicit. Incompatible/contracting migration rejects before any live mutation. Exact migration spec proves DB-secret file mount only, read-only root, cap-drop all, no-new-privileges, nonprivileged, no state/workspace/session mounts. Secret canary absent from inspect/config/output.

Stable codes: reuse D1_COLLECTION_NOT_READY for authority/compatibility/readiness. Invent no variant.

Size: hard <=400 net. Child 1 of exactly 2; child 2 consumes its authenticated evidence for live execution and host attestation.
notes:
2026-07-13: implementation dispatched after mandatory two-child split; hard <=400 net. Root-owned previous-core authority required; no caller-supplied previous image. Docker daemon + registered runsc/systrap only.

Binding compatibility probe: predecessor v0.1.84 and current main have identical auth/workspace-route blobs. On an isolated migrated DB and private Docker network with no published port, omit BORING_D1_HOST_ID only to disable production D1 wiring; exact production env is separately b2-attested. Boot new core, sign up through POST /auth/sign-up/email, PUT /api/v1/me/settings with a nonce marker, GET /api/v1/me to verify; boot predecessor on the same DB, sign in, verify marker, write/read a second marker; reboot new core, verify predecessor marker, then DELETE /api/v1/me for cleanup. This is the required app-level cross-version read/write proof; raw node-e SQL does not satisfy acceptance.

description:

title: D1-006 EU-host three-binding proof, DR rehearsal, and runbook
description:
## Goal and why
D1-006 EU-host three-binding proof, DR rehearsal, and runbook: Execute redacted EU-host proof for three workspaces/hostnames, N+1 continuity, rollback, isolation, secret canary, golden-path timing, and isolated backup/restore; write the bounded runbook.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Do not silently widen this bead.

## Acceptance and proof
Add focused unit tests at the public seam and integration/e2e or host-proof coverage where this task crosses a runtime boundary. Run the package typecheck, targeted tests, and the exact plan acceptance gate; attach redacted command output/evidence to the PR.

## Risks / owner blockers
Production exit is blocked by accepted D1-006a evidence and the owner EU privilege/profile decision; 15 minutes is measured target, not a gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
Focused unit coverage plus the named integration, e2e, or EU-host proof passes. Stable-code, redaction, authority, and no-widening assertions must hold before merge.
acceptance_criteria:
EU-host proof runs three independent P6-R bundles/workspaces/hostnames in one core revision; proves member/non-member/cross-binding denials before effects; N+1 continuity with retained identities byte-identical and no service restart; exact new-revision rollback removing only an unadmitted addition; stable-code failures for stale CAS, duplicates, root overlap, proxy confusion, unavailable secret, unsatisfied requirement, and partial readiness; D1-006a isolation suite plus secret canary; golden-path timing evidence; and isolated offline DR restore preserving admission/journal history, membership, digests, readable sessions, no ingress start, and recorded RPO/RTO. Requires accepted D1-006a evidence, D1-005c collection, and dated owner profile decision.

[Rescoped per Decision 25 (2026-07-14): prove the CORE only — three P6-R bundles/workspaces/hostnames in one revision, admission-ledger enforced, member/non-member/cross-binding denials, N+1 continuity, atomic-publish + exact rollback, sandbox isolation (iku + D1-006a suite) + secret canary, golden-path timing, isolated DR restore. The host-artifact attestation matrix (approved-release / host-security-config / Caddyfile / core-env digest attestation + migration-set evidence) is OUT OF SCOPE for v1 and defers with the layer behind gate wt-391-forward-tz4.]
notes: Soft coupling (Fable review, 2026-07-14): D1-006 should NOT be considered 'v1 done' without DEMAND-1 (wt-391-forward-yhc, five expert-comptable discovery conversations) underway. This is a SOFT gate for record-keeping only — do not hard-block this infra bead on sales; the coupling is tracked here, not enforced via br dep.

The HOST-ARTIFACT ATTESTATION MATRIX portion of the proof (observed approved-release/host-security-config/Caddyfile/core-env digest attestation, migration-set evidence binding) DEFERS WITH THE LAYER behind gate wt-391-forward-tz4 / consumed by wt-391-forward-nbu. title: SEC-1: prompt-injection & data-exfiltration review for vertical-agent document ingestion
description:
The real v1 attack surface has no bead. Adversarial review and hardening of the path where an agent ingests untrusted third-party documents — the accounting agent's invoice intake (per DEMAND-1's accounting vertical) is the canonical case, but this applies to any vertical agent's document-ingestion path. Two threats in scope: (1) prompt injection — malicious document content (invoice/PDF/email body) driving unintended tool calls; (2) data exfiltration — injected content causing leakage of other-workspace data or system/secret data via tool output or generated artifacts.

This is the actual v1 incident surface (untrusted-document-driven agent action), not host-attestation (which is deferred behind gate wt-391-forward-tz4 and is a lower-priority v1-plus concern).

## Scope
Threat model for the document-ingestion -> tool-call path; a malicious-document test corpus (injection payloads embedded in invoice-like documents/PDFs/text); and the mitigations: input isolation (untrusted document content never treated as instruction), tool-permission scoping (ingestion path cannot invoke tools outside its bounded set), and output validation (generated output/artifacts checked before returning to caller or across workspace boundaries).

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Not host-artifact-attestation (see wt-391-forward-tz4 gate — different, lower-priority v1-plus surface).

## Depends on
A first vertical agent existing (e.g. the accounting/invoice-intake demo agent referenced in GTM-CALLKITS.md) OR the M1 delivery path (landed, PR #650) providing a concrete ingestion surface to review against. No formal br dependency edge is added since neither is currently a single dispatchable bead in this tracker; this bead should not start until one of those surfaces is concretely buildable/built.

## Acceptance Criteria
A written threat model covering prompt-injection-via-document-content and cross-workspace/system data exfiltration; a malicious-document test corpus exercising both threats; and implemented mitigations (input isolation, tool-permission scoping, output validation) with tests proving each mitigation holds against the corpus. Stable-code, redaction, and no-widening assertions hold before merge.

title: Rename D1* codename prefix — strip planning codename from deployment identifiers
description:
Owner-flagged code smell (2026-07-15): ~139 D1*/d1* identifiers (D1AdmissionLedger, D1Plan, D1AgentArtifactSnapshot, D1ServerWiring, D1ActiveCollection, D1UserNeutralPreloader, etc.) in apps/full-app/src/server/deployment/. 'D1' is the #391 WORKSTREAM CODENAME (D1 = tenant-provisioning / multi-agent Docker host) leaking into permanent identifiers — uninformative (a reader doesn't know D1 = the host) and redundant with the deployment/ directory that already namespaces it.

SAFE TO RENAME: verified ZERO D1* names are in the published @hachej/boring-* package surface (all app-internal) — so no package API break; seneca and other consumers unaffected. It is a large mechanical rename (~139 identifiers, ~25 files) but contained to apps/full-app/deployment.

CONVENTION (recommended — drop the prefix, let deployment/ namespace it): D1AdmissionLedger -> AdmissionLedger, D1Plan -> DeploymentPlan, D1AgentArtifactSnapshot -> AgentArtifactSnapshot, D1ServerWiring -> deploymentServerWiring. Alternative (if a self-describing prefix is wanted for names that travel outside the dir): Host* / Deployment* (HostAdmissionLedger, HostPlan). Owner to confirm convention at dispatch if not drop-prefix.

TIMING: gate AFTER 3vt (the factory demo / phase-2 exit) — do NOT run mid-build; it would collide with active D1 branches. Mechanical rename only: no behavior change, preserve all types/logic; verify full build + typecheck + tests green + lint:invariants after. One PR (or a small stack if >400 lines/PR of pure renames — renames are cheap so a larger single PR is acceptable here with clear scoping). No package version bump (app-internal).

description:

title: D1-005c1 bounded collection prepare and stable-core controller
description:
Goal: implement the load-bearing in-process mechanism that prepares N independently resolved agent bundles and swaps one immutable live collection inside the stable core without disrupting retained bindings.

Scope: consume N independent injected P6-R resolutions; pre-read bundle sizes before runtime creation; enforce max bindings, per-bundle bytes, total bytes, and bounded concurrent preload. Build candidate-only runtime handles, atomically replace the complete immutable collection once, emit an exact served acknowledgement {revision,digest}, dispose failed/unused candidate handles, and preserve retained binding handles/identities across N+1. No first-effect admission rows are created during resolve/preload/all-ready.

Exclusions: no filesystem prepare/commit signal, durable pointer orchestration, ingress start, root command/main production wiring, scheduler/registry/control plane, host-artifact attestation, RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1, VerifiedD1HostExecution, approved-host-release ceremony, custom launcher, active replacement/removal.

Acceptance/proof: cap and cap+1 reject before runtime creation; per-bundle/total limits and bounded concurrency are proven; partial failure disposes only candidate handles and leaves old collection serving; one atomic swap exposes no mixed collection; exact served identity; retained request/session/reconnect handles survive N+1 byte-identical; preload creates zero admission rows/effects.

Stable codes: owns D1_COLLECTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED exactly; reuses D1_COLLECTION_NOT_READY. Invent no variants.

Decision 25: load-bearing core only. Hard <=400 total net lines including tests/scripts. Child 1 of exactly 2 for vup.
notes: 2026-07-14 implementation dispatched after Decision 25 two-child split. Hard <=400 total net. Build only bounded preload plus stable-core atomic collection controller; no durable signal/wiring (vup.2), no attestation layer or ceremony.

All 15 checks green. Proof: ordered package build, focused controller tests, full-app typecheck, E2E, remote-worker smoke, image smoke; final 343 net under hard 400. Multi-pass review fixed runtime-input identity drift, concurrent prepare authority, cleanup retry ownership, atomic size cache, and additive-only live-handle safety.

title: D1-005c2 durable root-core publication and wiring
description:
Goal: make the D1-005c1 stable-core collection swap durable and operable through the existing root command, atomic pointer, rollback journal, and ingress lifecycle.

Scope: add one fixed root-owned prepare/commit publication signal with exact pending identity validation. Map production command effects so preload prepares the candidate, durable publication commits the pointer, and verifyActive requests one core commit then requires the exact served {revision,digest} acknowledgement. Expose bounded redacted local status. Start ingress last on first boot only after exact served ack. Connect D1-004e removal recovery/unused-add rollback. Converge safely after lost prepare/commit signal, lost ack, or core restart; retain the old collection on failed prepare. Existing lazy first-effect admission remains the sole admission seam.

Exclusions: no resolver/provider registry, scheduler/control plane, caller-triggered activation, host-artifact attestation, RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1, VerifiedD1HostExecution, approved-host-release ceremony, d1_proof database, custom launcher, active replacement/removal, retained-session disruption.

Acceptance/proof: failed prepare leaves old collection serving; durable pointer publication occurs before exactly one swap; exact ack is required before ingress first start; no mixed collection is observable; restart/lost-signal/lost-ack recovery converges idempotently; unused unadmitted addition rolls back as a new revision while any admitted use rejects removal; status and failures expose no secret/raw path.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED, D1_ROLLBACK_JOURNAL_FAILED, D1 admission codes; owns no new code.

Decision 25: load-bearing core only. Hard <=400 total net lines including tests/scripts. Child 2 of exactly 2; depends on vup.1.
notes: 2026-07-14 dispatched after vup.1/#754 merge. Implement durable root/core prepare-commit signal, exact served ack, production dependency wiring, ingress-last, and D1-004e recovery/rollback handoff only. Decision 25 core path: no RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1, VerifiedD1HostExecution, approved-host-release/artifact/env/Caddy attestation or ceremony. Hard <=400 total net.

title: D1-005c2a core publication protocol and served lifecycle
description:
Goal: make the stable core the atomic serving authority through a fixed root-triggerable but non-authoritative Unix-socket prepare/commit/status protocol.

Scope: add canonical root-owned D1PendingPublicationV1 identity at the fixed host-state path and a bounded fixed Unix socket at /run/boring/d1-control/publication.sock. Socket payload carries only action + operationId; every prepare/commit re-reads immutable revision, pending record and durable pointer. Core owns an in-memory served-active reader used by host surface, landing, readiness and admission. Prepare validates exact expected/target/diff and preloads without changing served state. Commit requires the same pending identity and durable target pointer, performs one vup.1 atomic serve, swaps served-active and returns exact redacted status {durableRevision,servedRevision,pendingOperation}. Restart converges before public listen for expected+pending or target+pending; unrelated tuples fail closed. Extend vup.1 only for D1-004e-authorized unused rollback: exact sorted removal set, retained handles live through prepare, removed handles dispose only after target serve.

Exclusions: no root writer/client, command effects, journal mutation, ingress operation, registry/scheduler/control plane, caller activation, attestation/runtime evidence/approved-release ceremony, custom launcher, active replacement or secret/runtime rotation.

Acceptance/proof: additive prepare leaves served reader unchanged; commit exposes all N atomically; rollback retains removed handle until commit then disposes; invalid socket frame/credentials/op/digest/diff fails; bounded framing/mode; candidate cleanup; lost signal/ack retry; restart at expected and target states; request/landing/readiness/admission use only served-active authority.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED only; no new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Grandchild 1 of 2 under vup.2.
notes: 2026-07-14 dispatched after nested two-part recut. Socket uses a distinct D1_CONTROL_ROOT=/run/boring/d1-control/<hostId> host bind to /run/boring/d1-control because /run/boring/d1 remains read-only; root creates the directory before core start, with root-owned parent and only app-group traversal/write for the socket leaf. The socket is untrusted wake-up only and grants no state/revision write. Acceptance requires a real full-app preloadBinding/handle adapter and the vup.1 served in-memory reader to back surface, landing, readiness and admission; fake handles or leaving routing on the durable pointer are stop-signs. Hard <=400 total net; no attestation layer.

title: D1-005c2a0 deployed-agent runtime recipe

Full-app adapter reads local immutable bundleRef/deploymentRef, calls the existing deployment resolver, and requires exact persisted binding identities/digests. D1 runtime scope keys include resolvedDigest and append only verified selected instructions to that binding prompt.

Exclusions: no registry/cache/remote fetch/watcher/hot reload/catalog, tool/skill/plugin selection, persistence, runtime handles/preload, pointer/socket/admission, auth decision, attestation/ceremony, new package.

Acceptance/proof: two immutable bundle/deployment pairs produce distinct exact recipes and prompts; default deployment plus all digests reverify; malformed/ref escape/wrong digest fails before runtime creation; siblings receive only their own instructions; same inputs deep-equal.

Stable codes: preserve AGENT_DEFINITION_INVALID/AGENT_DEPLOYMENT_INVALID; D1 boundary reuses D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED. No new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Leaf A0.
notes: 2026-07-14 dispatched after production-seam audit. Implement exact immutable deployed-agent recipe consumed by routes; no registry workaround, runtime handles, pointer/socket or attestation. Stop if local immutable bundle/deployment ref layout is not fixed. Hard <=400 total net.

title: D1-005c2a-pre immutable agent artifact snapshot
description:
Goal: define and materialize the minimal immutable local agent artifact contract required to turn opaque bundle/deployment refs into executable revision assets without a registry.

Scope: fixed root input slot /etc/boring/d1/agent-artifacts/<hostId>/<bindingId>/artifact.json. Refs are never path segments; the strict envelope embeds and must exactly match plan hostId/bindingId/bundleRef/deploymentRef plus existing CompiledAgentBundle and AgentDeployment schemas. Root reader enforces fixed ownership/mode, exact entries, regular singly-linked files, no symlink/traversal/change-during-read, UTF-8/JSON and collection byte/count limits. Re-resolve and compare all identities/digests, then copy canonical envelopes into <revision-temp>/agent-artifacts/<bindingId>.json before the existing whole-revision fsync+atomic rename. Revision copies are core-readable 0440 under 0710 and self-contained for rollback. Export only fixed input load and revision artifact read; no list/register/update/delete API.

Exclusions: no generic registry/cache/watcher/remote fetch/blob store/GC, approved-host-release or host-artifact/env attestation, d1_proof/release ceremony, runtime handles/routes/pointer/socket/secrets/custom launcher.

Acceptance/proof: opaque refs including bundle@1 never select paths; valid round-trip; traversal/symlink/hardlink/wrong owner/mode/extra/change/oversize/tampered definition/deployment reject before candidate rename; failed write leaves no visible revision; successful and rollback revisions read their own exact artifact; core can read but not write.

Stable codes: boundary reuses D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED; preserve internal agent definition/deployment parser codes. No new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Prerequisite for A0.
notes: 2026-07-14 dispatched as A0 prerequisite. Use fixed binding-id input slots and self-contained revision snapshots; opaque bundleRef/deploymentRef remain data, never path segments. Existing trusted deployment/operator side stages compiler output; root command revalidates under host mutation lock. No registry or attestation ceremony. Hard <=400 total net.

title: D1-005c2a1 disposable host runtime preloader
description:
Goal: expose the real agent-route runtime binding lifecycle to trusted host boot code with candidate ownership and deterministic cleanup.

Scope: add WorkspaceAgentRuntimePreloader.prepare(recipe) returning an idempotent PreparedWorkspaceAgentRuntime handle. Use the exact A0 runtime scope/createRuntimeBinding/RuntimeBindingLifecycle path used by requests. Track whether candidate preparation created/shared/pre-existed; dispose retires only candidate-owned entries, releases retained entries, and waits existing leases. Core forwards the same in-process preloader instance.

Exclusions: no D1 imports in agent/core, pointer/socket/status, registry/manager class, caller API/auth bypass, scheduler/new cache, replacement/hot reload, attestation.

Acceptance/proof: exact recipe-keyed real binding; duplicate prepare shares one creation; failed candidate disposes once; retained entry is not retired; active lease drains before retirement; post-retirement creates fresh; core callback receives the same preloader used by routes; no fabricated request/user credential.

Stable codes: existing runtime/lifecycle errors; D1 boundary later uses D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED. No new code. Hard <=400 total net. Leaf A1, depends A0.

title: D1-005c2a2 served collection authority and rollback
description:
Goal: make one in-memory immutable collection the serving authority and support only exact D1-004e-authorized unused-add rollback.

Scope: add D1ServedCollectionAuthority over landed controller, A0 recipes and A1 handles. Its reader backs hostSurface, landing, readiness, effect admission and admission-ledger rereads; disk active reader becomes bootstrap/artifact validation only. Prepare/commit/discard expose exact served ack/status. Extend controller removal only for kind=rollback with exact sorted removal set matching active→target diff; retained handles stay live through prepare and removed handles retire only after atomic commit.

Exclusions: no pending writer/socket/control bind/restart transport, root command/compose/journal mutation, attestation, active replacement/secret/runtime rotation, generic removal.

Acceptance/proof: durable pointer mutation alone changes none of the five serving consumers; all flip together only on commit; additive N+1 retains handles/in-flight lease; failed candidate disposes additions only; exact rollback delays removed disposal until commit; wrong/unsorted set or replacement rejects; prepare creates no admission row; no partial collection is observable.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED, D1_ACTIVE_BINDING_RESTART_REQUIRED and collection-limit code. No new code. Hard <=400 total net. Leaf B, depends A1.
close_reason: Consolidated into wt-391-forward-vup.2.3 (D1-005c2a1-2 durable publication core: preloader + served authority + rollback) per owner ruling 2026-07-14 — anti-fragmentation. Scope preserved verbatim in the new bead; no work lost.

title: D1-005c2a3 Unix publication protocol and restart convergence
description:
Goal: expose served collection authority to root through a bounded untrusted Unix wake-up protocol and reconstruct exact served state after core restart.

Scope: canonical read-only-to-core D1PendingPublicationV1 at fixed root state path; fixed bounded one-request socket /run/boring/d1-control/publication.sock on distinct D1_CONTROL_ROOT bind. Payload carries only prepare/commit/status + operationId; every action rereads pending, immutable revisions and durable pointer. Prepare requires served=expected; commit requires same pending and durable=target then serves exact target. Restart before listen reconstructs no-pending durable state, expected+pending with target prepared, or target+pending served ack; unrelated tuple fails closed. Status is redacted {durableRevision,servedRevision,pendingOperation}.

Exclusions: no root client/writer, command effects, ingress, journal recovery, daemon/HTTP activation, credentials/custom launcher, attestation, second pointer. Root creates control directory in later leaf.

Acceptance/proof: bounded frame/one request and fixed modes; payload cannot select path/digest; invalid pending/path/digest rejects; prepare leaves served unchanged; commit requires durable target; lost signal/ack retries; restart convergence for all valid tuples; unrelated tuple fails; status leaks no paths/secrets; state mounts remain read-only and only control leaf writable.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED; no new code. Hard <=400 total net. Leaf C, depends B.
close_reason: Consolidated into wt-391-forward-vup.2.4 (D1-005c2a3+2b Unix publication protocol, restart convergence, and root ingress-last wiring) per owner ruling 2026-07-14 — anti-fragmentation. Scope preserved verbatim in the new bead; no work lost.

title: D1-005c2b root command ingress-last and recovery wiring
description:
Goal: drive the core publication protocol from the existing root D1 command, start ingress only after exact served acknowledgement, and bind D1-004e recovery completion to served state.

Scope: root atomically installs the canonical pending record, sends prepare and requires served==expected. Map effects.preload to prepare. After durable pointer publication, map verifyActive to commit and require exact durable=target, served=target, pending=operationId before writing idle. First boot compose runs migration then core-app only; exact ingress up --no-deps runs only after first served ack. Extend D1-004e activation/recovery sequence to prepared journal -> durable target pointer -> exact core commit/ack -> terminal committed. Recovery consults core status: expected/expected retries publish+commit; target/expected retries commit; target/target finalizes; unrelated tuple fails closed. Admission fences remain landed; retries idempotent; N+1 never recreates/restarts core.

Exclusions: no resolver/provider registry, scheduler/control plane, caller activation, attestation/runtime evidence/approved-release ceremony, d1_proof, custom launcher, active replacement/removal beyond unused D1-004e rollback, force-recreate or core restart.

Acceptance/proof: prepare -> pointer -> served ack order; failed prepare leaves pointer and served old; lost ack/core restart converge via status; rollback crash injection at every phase; used removal rejects; ingress cannot start before exact first ack and retry targets only exact service; N+1 does not invoke compose/core recreation; status/errors expose no paths or secrets.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED and D1_ROLLBACK_JOURNAL_FAILED plus admission codes; no new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Grandchild 2 of 2, depends on vup.2.1.
close_reason: Consolidated into wt-391-forward-vup.2.4 (D1-005c2a3+2b Unix publication protocol, restart convergence, and root ingress-last wiring) per owner ruling 2026-07-14 — anti-fragmentation. Scope preserved verbatim in the new bead; no work lost.

title: D1-005c2a1-2 durable publication core: preloader + served authority + rollback
description:
